page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.u2a-c1m2.16xlarge is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 64 dedicated physical cores on the x86_64 architecture, utilizing AMD EPYC processors running at a clock speed of 2.25 GHz. The system is configured with a single thread per core, providing a direct one-to-one mapping of virtual CPUs to physical cores.

The instance is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, which translates to a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or integrated GPU hardware, requiring external block storage solutions for data persistence. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 16 Gbps, and the instance does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Given the 1:2 ratio of vCPUs to memory, this instance is designed for compute-heavy workloads that require high parallel processing capabilities without massive memory footprints. This hardware profile is qualitatively cost-efficient for distributed computing, batch processing, and CPU-bound application servers where resource density is optimized for raw processing power rather than memory capacity.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.u2a-c1m2.16xlarge is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance featuring 64 dedicated AMD EPYC physical cores on the x86_64 architecture. Operating at a clock speed of 2.25 GHz with a single thread per core, the instance provides 128.0 GB of system memory, resulting in a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, relying on external network-attached storage. Network performance is anchored by a baseline bandwidth of 16 Gbps. This hardware configuration is designed for parallel processing tasks, batch computing, and CPU-bound workloads that benefit from a high core count and a balanced, compute-focused memory ratio.

tagline: A high-core compute-optimized instance featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors and a balanced two-gigabyte-per-core memory configuration.
